note: SpamSpan is a core-Filter-dependent email obfuscation module. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) and probe SpamSpan-specific facts: it is configured as a text-format filter (no dedicated settings page; configure route is core filter.admin_overview), the filter plugin id and its no-JS fallback markup, the 8 filter settings incl. the contact-form mode, the email_spamspan field formatter for Email fields, and the spamspan service / Twig filter for code. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the SpamSpan module enabled, how do I actually turn on email-address obfuscation for content? There is no SpamSpan settings page in the admin menu — where do I configure it?
evals.expected_output: SpamSpan has no dedicated settings page; its configure route is core's filter.admin_overview. Edit a text format at Admin > Configuration > Content authoring > Text formats and editors (/admin/config/content/formats), enable the 'SpamSpan email address encoding filter', order it after HTML-generating filters, set its options under Filter settings, and save.

evals.prompt: In SpamSpan 3.2.x, what is the machine id of the text-format filter plugin, and what filter type is it (does it change text irreversibly)?
evals.expected_output: The filter plugin id is filter_spamspan (Drupal\spamspan\Plugin\Filter\FilterSpamspan), type TYPE_TRANSFORM_IRREVERSIBLE.

evals.prompt: With SpamSpan, what does a visitor see for an email address when JavaScript is disabled, and how is the address made clickable when JavaScript is on? Which setting changes the '@' replacement text?
evals.expected_output: Without JavaScript the address degrades to a readable form like 'me [at] example.com' (the '@' text is the spamspan_at setting, default ' [at] '); with JavaScript the spamspan/obfuscate library reassembles the <span class="spamspan"> markup into a real mailto: link.

evals.prompt: I want SpamSpan to link email addresses to a contact form instead of exposing a mailto: address at all. Which filter setting enables that, and how do I override the form URL and link text for a single address written in the content?
evals.expected_output: Enable spamspan_use_form ('Use a form instead of a link'); it uses spamspan_form_pattern with spamspan_form_default_url / spamspan_form_default_displaytext. Per-address overrides use the bracket syntax user@example.com[mycontactform|Contact me using this form].

evals.prompt: I have a core Email field on a content type and want its value shown as an obfuscated (spam-safe) link. Which display formatter do I choose in Manage display with SpamSpan installed?
evals.expected_output: Choose the 'Email SpamSpan' formatter (id email_spamspan) on the Email field in Manage display; it obfuscates the value via the spamspan service and supports the same options as the filter.

evals.prompt: In custom Drupal code or a Twig template, how do I obfuscate an arbitrary string containing email addresses with SpamSpan? Name the service and the Twig filter.
evals.expected_output: Call the 'spamspan' service: \Drupal::service('spamspan')->spamspan($text, $settings) which returns obfuscated HTML (attach the spamspan/obfuscate library yourself). In Twig use the filter {{ text|spamspan }}, which also attaches the library automatically.

evals.prompt: What modules does SpamSpan 3.2.x depend on, and does it define its own permission to configure it, or does it reuse a core permission?
evals.expected_output: It depends only on core's Filter module (drupal:filter) and defines no permissions of its own; configuring/testing it is gated by core's 'administer filters' permission.
